title: Asclepias tapalpana
taxonomy: Asclepias tapalpana is classified within the family Apocynaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. It belongs to the subfamily Asclepiadoideae, which comprises the milkweed and related species. Asclepias tapalpana is further classified within the genus Asclepias, which includes around 150 species of flowering plants. The exact classification of Asclepias tapalpana may vary depending on the source and the taxonomic system being used. As a result, Asclepias tapalpana is also sometimes classified within the subfamily Periplocoideae.
uses: Asclepias tapalpana has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. It is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant's roots are also believed to have anti-diarrheal properties, making it a potential treatment for gastrointestinal issues.
